headline: T-Mobile Tuesdays Revamp Puts Customer Retention And TMUS Valuation In Focus
summary: T-Mobile US (NasdaqGS:TMUS) is expanding its T-Mobile Tuesdays rewards program for its 10-year anniversary. The refreshed perks include a free MLB.TV subscription and new merchandise offers for eligible customers. The move comes as T-Mobile responds to higher churn and customer pushback on recent price and plan changes. T-Mobile US, trading at $208.47, is leaning on its decade old T-Mobile Tuesdays program as a tool to keep subscribers engaged and reduce switching. The stock shows mixed...

headline: T-Mobile, Verizon, AT&T Go All-In On Discounts As Churn Surge Hits
summary: Promotional activity among the "Big 3" U.S. carriers—AT&T Inc., Verizon Communications Inc. and T-Mobile US, Inc.—is gaining momentum heading into the second quarter of 2026, reflecting intensifying competition in the postpaid market. Elevated churn remains a key driver, as many subscribers roll off 36-month financing plans and re-enter the market for device upgrades. headline: T-Mobile makes free perk lineup even more generous for customers
summary: One feature that has set T-Mobile apart from its rivals is its T-Mobile Tuesdays loyalty program, which delivers weekly customer rewards via the T-Life app. In 2025, the program gave customers more than $900 worth of freebies, giveaways, and exclusive perks. After surpassing 2.3 million users, ...
